summary: The corrected plan has no remaining blockers across reality, architecture, quality, or systems review. It preserves the original review artifact, resolves every validated blocker/major/substantive minor, and adds live-evidence corrections for the session schema, Scenario A, create-only task definitions, and local-auth acceptance.

warnings.issue: Task 1 has a broad validation-signature propagation surface.
warnings.resolution: Use one shared adapter, thread the authoritative catalog through every enumerated caller, run the dedicated cross-seam matrix and exact legacy nodes, and review the whole diff once after Task 4B.

warnings.issue: The external Scenario A package and AWS environment require strict phase ownership.
warnings.resolution: Leave the dirty package untouched, work in a detached external worktree, use append-only create-only task definitions, rebind and verify every phase, hold an ETag-conditional deployment lease, and abort on foreign events or any destructive/out-of-allowlist plan.

warnings.issue: Release-wide trust-tier fingerprints already differ at the target head.
warnings.resolution: Keep the captured pre-Task-1 canonical artifact immutable and require byte-for-byte equality after Task 4B; never regenerate or rotate the release baseline in this work.

verified_corrections: PluginAvailabilitySnapshot has no invented unavailable_reason method; the plan indexes PluginAvailability.reason.value.
All existing focused pytest node IDs in the corrected package collect successfully; the corrected exact subset collected 15 parametrized cases with exit code 0.
The migration script is explicitly copied into the runtime image and shares one exported PostgreSQL trigger-DDL authority with metadata creation.
Scenario A uses workspace acceptance-a and a detached state-aligned Terraform worktree.
Timeout and candidate task definitions are separately addressed create-only resources; no replacement is allowed.
Maintenance listener-rule state is Terraform-modeled, so post-DDL fix-forward candidates can be registered without hiding drift.
Route-level concurrent replay and direct PostgreSQL stale-state behavior are distinguished correctly.
Local-auth Playwright evidence is same-origin, in-memory, artifact-safe, and preserves the successful splice session.
